zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Houghton於1535年出生在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰時，她的父親John Houghton36歲，母親Margerie Colmer35歲。她於1560年12月15日與William Bradley在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾瑟格利結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她於1620年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州。她去世於1610年在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾易阿爾斯 巴頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Houghton于1535年出生在英国奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰时，她的父亲John Houghton36岁，母亲Margerie Colmer35岁。她于1560年12月15日与William Bradley在英国英格兰斯塔福爾瑟格利结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她于1620年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州。她去世于1610年在英国奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰享年75岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾易阿爾斯 巴頓。

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Houghton was born in 1535, in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, her father, John Houghton, was 36 and her mother, Margerie Colmer, was 35. She married William Bradley on 15 December 1560, in Sedgley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She immigrated to Virginia, British Colonial America in 1620. She died in 1610, in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 75, and was buried in Earls Barton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.Stanton St. John, Oxfordshire, England,: Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Houghton est née en 1535, à Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, John Houghton, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Margerie Colmer, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé William Bradley le 15 décembre 1560, à Sedgley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle a immigré au Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1620. Elle est décédée en 1610, à Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterrée à Earls Barton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Houghton 1535 in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Houghton 36 und ihre Mutter Margerie Colmer 35. Sie hat am 15. Dezember 1560 in Sedgley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom William Bradley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie ist 1620 nach Virginia, British Colonial America eingewandert. Sie starb 1610 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England und wurde in Earls Barton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Houghton è nata il nel 1535 nel Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, sua padre, John Houghton, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Margerie Colmer, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato William Bradley il 15 dicembre 1560, nel Sedgley, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Immigrata nel Virginia, British Colonial America nel 1620. Morì nel 1610, nel Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolta a Earls Barton, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Houghtonが1535年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン スト ジョンで生まれた時，父John Houghtonは36歳，母Margerie Colmerは35歳であった。1560年12月15日にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアセドグリーでWilliam Bradleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1620年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州に移住した。彼女は1610年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン スト ジョンで75歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアエアルルズ バートンにて埋葬された。

ko.Stanton St. John, Oxfordshire, England,: 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 스트 존
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Houghton(이)가 1535년 영국 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 스트 존에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Houghton(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margerie Colmer(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1560년 12월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 세지리에서 William Bradley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1620년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주로 이민을 떠났다. 그녀는 1610년 영국 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 스트 존에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 에를스 바턴에 묻혔다.

pt.Roanoke, Virginia, USA: Roanoke, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América
pt.Stanton St. John, Oxfordshire, England,: Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Earls Barton, Northamptonshire, England: Earls Barton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Houghton nasceu em 1535, em Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Houghton, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Margerie Colmer, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com William Bradley em 15 de dezembro de 1560, em Sedgley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela imigrou para o Virginia, British Colonial America em 1620. Ela faleceu em 1610, em Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, com 75 anos, e foi sepultada em Earls Barton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Stanton St. John, Oxfordshire, England,: стантон ст джон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Houghton родилась в 1535 г. в местности стантон ст джон, оксфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Houghton было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Margerie Colmer, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Bradley 15 декабря 1560 г. в местности седжли, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она иммигрировала в Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1620 году. Она умерла в 1610 г. в местности стантон ст джон, оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности эарлс бартон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Stanton St. John, Oxfordshire, England,: Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.Earls Barton, Northamptonshire, England: Earls Barton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Houghton nació en 1535, en Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Houghton, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Margerie Colmer, tenía 35 años. Se casó con William Bradley el 15 de diciembre de 1560, en Sedgley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Inmigró a Virginia, British Colonial America en 1620. Murió en 1610, en Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultada en Earls Barton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
